summary: Decompiled and typed a large slice of lb audio/background-flash code. The PR body states that lbbgflash.c and lbaudio_ax.c match; the decomp.dev report confirms 4 new full matches and 10 large unmatched-function improvements, with most work in src/melee/lb/lbaudio_ax.c. The change replaces many UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS prototypes with concrete signatures, introduces lbAudioAx_UserData, adds needed baselib/camera/fighter/item includes, and adds static headers for several vi units so globals and constants can be declared cleanly.
what_changed: Implemented many previously stubbed lbaudio_ax functions, including fn_80023254, fn_800251EC, fn_80025B44, fn_80025CBC, fn_80025E38, lbAudioAx_800263E8, lbAudioAx_800264E4, lbAudioAx_80026510, fn_800267B0, fn_800268B4, lbAudioAx_80026F2C, and lbAudioAx_8002702C.
Reworked lbAudioAx_800265C4 to use typed lbAudioAx_UserData fields instead of raw int* indexing and M2C_FIELD access to HSD_GObj_Entities.
Added lbAudioAx_UserData in lbaudio_ax.static.h with recovered offsets for entity, start/end values, pan fields, voice_id, current_frame, and end_frame.
Added a local SoundParams struct in lbaudio_ax.c for lbAudioAx_800263E8 setup before GObj creation and userdata initialization.
Added baselib gobjproc/gobjuserdata, camera, ftlib, and item includes used by the new typed audio code.
Implemented lbbgflash functions fn_800204C8 and fn_80021C1C, including background flash state update logic and ColorOverlay application through lb_80014498.
Updated public headers to replace unknown signatures with concrete void, bool, HSD_GObj*, HSD_AObj*, and typed-argument prototypes.
Added vi0801.static.h, vi1201v2.static.h, and vi1202.static.h containing extern/static declarations for scene, archive, JObj, GXColor, Vec3, and float/double globals.
smart_moves: Recovered lbAudioAx_UserData first, then used that struct to simplify audio object traversal, voice key-off behavior, pan updates, and userdata frees.

Converted HSD_GObj_Entities access from byte-offset M2C_FIELD style to array indexing, e.g. ((HSD_GObj**) HSD_GObj_Entities)[0x3E], making the GObj list traversal more readable while preserving the slot evidence.
Used camera bounds plus ftLib_80086644 and it_8026B294 to model positional audio panning for fighter and item entities instead of leaving the function as raw pointer logic.
Preserved matching-sensitive control flow with explicit gotos, loop shapes, manual unrolling, and integer/floating-point casts where the diff suggests they were needed for close or exact codegen.
Separated static/internal declarations into .static.h files, including new vi static headers, rather than overloading public headers with file-local globals.
Kept still-unknown functions as address-based names while improving only signatures where evidence was available, avoiding speculative semantic names.
decomp_lessons: For lb audio work, recovering a userdata layout can unlock many functions at once because GObj lifecycle, pan calculation, voice_id lookup, and object freeing all share the same offsets.
When replacing raw array/pointer indexing, retain exact field offsets in comments or struct padding; this PR's lbAudioAx_UserData documents the 0x48-byte allocation and critical field offsets.
Header cleanup should accompany implementation work: concrete prototypes in lbaudio_ax.h and lbbgflash.h make later callsite matching easier and reduce UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS spread.

GObj audio object management depends on correct creation, userdata initialization, proc attachment, and cleanup order: GObj_Create, HSD_ObjAlloc, GObj_InitUserData, HSD_GObjProc_8038FD54, and HSD_GObjPLink_80390228 appear together in lbAudioAx_800263E8.
Background flash decomp can rely on recovered state structs but may still need matching-oriented gotos for switch/range logic.
decomp.dev results can distinguish exact matches from partial improvements; this PR had several near matches around 97-99% even when the PR body broadly said the files match.
naming_conventions: Kept unknown address-based function names such as fn_80023254, fn_800251EC, fn_80025B44, fn_800267B0, and fn_800268B4.
Used module-prefixed exported names like lbAudioAx_800263E8, lbAudioAx_800264E4, lbAudioAx_80026510, and lbBgFlash_80021C48.
Named recovered userdata as lbAudioAx_UserData, matching the module prefix and making later typed replacements clear.

Used descriptive local names for recovered behavior where semantics were clear, including entity, voice_id, pan_left, pan_right, current_frame, end_frame, cam_left, cam_center, cam_right, start_val, and end_val.
Static/global labels remain address-style lbl_804337C4, lbl_804338A4, lbl_80433B44, lbl_804D6438, etc., when semantic names were not evidenced.
New vi static headers use address-derived include guards such as __GALE01_31EFE4, __GALE01_3204C0, and __GALE01_321014.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used explicit goto labels such as ret_true, set_flag, check_flag, store_pan, insert, loop_body, and loop_check to match original branch structure.
Used manual unrolling in fn_80023254 for initializing and shifting arrays, likely to preserve codegen around 0x38-element tables.
Used exact integer constants and masks in audio bank/category logic, including 0x37 element loops, 0x40 center pan, 0x7F max pan, 0x83D60 guard, and 64-bit masks such as 0x0048000000000003ULL.
Used typed casts for entity kind checks, e.g. reading *(u16*) entity and switching on type values 4 and 6 before calling fighter/item position helpers.
Kept floating-point interpolation explicit with f32 casts and clamp logic for panning and value ramps.
Retained NULL checks and cleanup gotos around GObj/userdata allocation to preserve exact object lifecycle behavior.
Used local temporary structs such as SoundParams to match stack layout for calls into still-unmatched fn_80025FAC.
review_feedback: No human review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
decomp.dev bot reported matched code increased to 48.56% (+0.02%, +664 bytes).
decomp.dev bot reported 4 new full matches: lbAudioAx_8002702C (+316 bytes), fn_800267B0 (+260 bytes), fn_80021C1C (+44 bytes), and lbAudioAx_800264E4 (+44 bytes).

decomp.dev bot reported 10 improvements in unmatched functions, including fn_800251EC at 97.02%, fn_80025CBC at 98.32%, fn_80025B44 at 98.30%, lbAudioAx_800263E8 at 99.92%, and lbAudioAx_80026510 at 99.60%.
decomp.dev bot reported one small .sdata2 regression in lbaudio_ax, from 70.59% to 69.44% (-1 byte).
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that finish matching fn_800251EC, fn_80025B44, fn_80025CBC, fn_80025E38, lbAudioAx_800263E8, lbAudioAx_80026510, lbAudioAx_80026F2C, or fn_800268B4.
Search for subsequent renames or refinements of lbAudioAx_UserData fields x0, xC, x20, x2C, and x3C.
Check whether SoundParams was later moved from a local typedef into a shared/static header with a stable name.
Search for PRs addressing the reported lbaudio_ax .sdata2 regression.
Find later vi0801, vi1201v2, or vi1202 work that reuses the new .static.h global declarations.
